query: How frequently does the CDC issue new Travel Health Notices for North American countries (Mexico, Canada, Caribbean, Central America)? List recent CDC travel health notices issued in 2025 and 2026 for North American countries with their issue dates. What is the typical monthly rate of new notices affecting North America?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings:

**How often CDC issues North American Travel Health Notices:**
- CDC uses a 3-level THN system and updates notices "regularly based on the most current data."
- Many notices are **regional/global** (Global Dengue, Global Measles, Oropouche in the Americas, Global Polio), so they blanket-cover many North American destinations at once.

**Recent North America-relevant THNs (2025–2026), with dates:**
- Diphtheria in Haiti — Level 1 — Jun 25, 2026
- Hepatitis A in Canada (Manitoba) — Level 1 — Jun 4, 2026
- Chikungunya in French Guiana — Level 2 — Jun 4, 2026
- Rocky Mountain Spotted Fever in Mexico — Level 1 — updated Dec 23, 2025
- Oropouche in the Americas (Cuba, Panama) — Level 1 — updated Sep 18, 2025
- Yellow Fever in Colombia — Level 2 — Sep 2, 2025
- Global Measles — Level 1 — updated May 28, 2025
- Global Dengue — Level 1 — updated regularly
- Salmonella Newport in Mexico — Level 1 — still active (orig. May 30, 2024)
- Plus New World Screwworm (Mexico/Central America) via HAN advisory Jan 2026, and Caribbean flooding notices (Hurricane Melissa) late 2025.

**Typical monthly rate:**
- Roughly **6–9 distinct North America-relevant THNs** were newly posted or materially updated over the ~13 months from mid-2025 to July 2026 → approximately **0.5–1 per month (about one every 1–2 months)**, clustering in spring–summer vector-borne season.
- **Caveat:** CDC publishes no official "North America monthly rate"; these are derived counts sensitive to how one treats regional/global vs. country-specific notices and updates vs. new postings.

sources.title: Global Dengue - Level 1 - CDC
sources.snippet: THN originally posted June 20, 2024, updated regularly; dengue is a year-round risk with outbreaks every 2-5 years.

sources.title: CDC warns about New World screwworm just across the border in Mexico | CIDRAP
sources.snippet: CDC issued a Health Alert Network (HAN) advisory on New World screwworm animal cases in Tamaulipas, Mexico (as of January 20, 2026).

sources.title: New World Screwworm: Outbreak Moves into Northern Mexico | HAN | CDC
sources.snippet: CDC HAN advisory HAN00526 on New World screwworm moving into northern Mexico.

sources.title: CDC Issues Urgent Advisory As Dengue Fever Cases Surge In Mexico And 19 Other Countries
sources.snippet: CDC dengue travel advisory covering Mexico and 19 other countries, updated May 2025; over 13 million dengue cases across the Americas.

sources.title: Cuba's Chikungunya Crisis Poses Risks for Visitors
sources.snippet: Coverage of chikungunya risk in Cuba (Dec 2025), related to CDC/Caribbean travel guidance.
